pr_summary: Add order cancellation and status polling.
verdict_reason: The diff contains high-risk findings that should be fixed before merge.

findings.title: Unbounded polling loop
findings.description: The polling loop has no timeout, max attempts, or cancellation path.
findings.suggestion: Add a deadline, max retry count, or cancellation signal.

findings.line_content: export async function cancelOrder(orderId: string, userId: string): Promise<void> {
findings.title: Authorization check missing for sensitive operation
findings.description: The code performs a sensitive mutation without a visible ownership, role, or token validation check.
findings.suggestion: Check that the authenticated caller is allowed to perform this operation before changing state.

findings.title: Possible null dereference after lookup
findings.description: A lookup result is dereferenced without a visible not-found guard.
findings.suggestion: Check for a missing result before reading fields from the object.

findings.title: Missing null check for order.userEmail
findings.description: The order.userEmail is used without checking if it's null or undefined.
findings.suggestion: Add a null check before sending the notification.

findings.title: Polling loop may not terminate
findings.description: The polling loop may not terminate if the order status is not updated correctly, leading to an infinite loop.
findings.suggestion: Add a test to verify the polling loop terminates correctly.

findings.title: Await inside loop serializes independent work
findings.description: Awaiting inside the loop makes independent operations run one after another instead of concurrently.
findings.suggestion: Use Promise.all or a bounded concurrency helper for independent work.

findings.title: Unknown map key can produce invalid result
findings.description: A dynamic key is read from a map without validating that the key exists.
findings.suggestion: Validate the key or provide an explicit fallback before calculating.

findings.line_content: export async function cancelOrder(orderId: string, userId: string): Promise<void> {
findings.title: State transition side effects need tests
findings.description: The diff changes persisted state and triggers an external side effect, but does not show tests for repeated transitions or side-effect failure.
findings.suggestion: Add tests for repeated or invalid state transitions and side-effect failure handling.

findings.title: Potential busy-waiting issue
findings.description: The pollOrderStatus function uses a busy-waiting approach, which can lead to high CPU usage.
findings.suggestion: Consider using a more efficient approach, such as using a timer or a separate thread.

findings.title: Bulk cancellation edge case not tested
findings.description: The bulk cancellation function does not have tests for edge cases such as an empty orderIds array or a null orderIds array.
findings.suggestion: Add tests for bulk cancellation edge cases.

findings.line_content: const discounts: any = { SAVE10: 0.1, SAVE20: 0.2, SAVE50: 0.5 };
findings.title: Avoid any for newly added value
findings.description: The new code uses `any`, which bypasses TypeScript type checking.
findings.suggestion: Replace `any` with a specific type, union, or safer unknown shape.

positive_observations: The diff is small enough for a focused typescript line-level review.
The changes are organized around clear functions, which makes targeted fixes straightforward.
missing_tests: Add a test to verify the polling loop terminates correctly.
Add tests for repeated or invalid state transitions and side-effect failure handling.
Add tests for bulk cancellation edge cases.
